1.Pull 2 cases for sampling for each batch.

against a white background in a well-lit area. Send photo to
Birgitta.Caspersen@ejgallo.com
  1.Make sure to note the location within the case of each of the bottles. Use
the provided file to fill in the pass/fail grid.
3.Inspect each bottle for color changes by holding up bottle against provided
Color Guide (mailed to each RDC). If you no longer have the color guides that
were previously sent, please reach out to Birgitta Caspersen for an additional
copy to be sent.
  1.There is a clear line defining pass vs fail on the Guide, anything falling
to the left of the line is a “pass”.
  2.Please take photos of the bottles that failed and send to
Birgitta.Caspersen@ejgallo.com.
4.If there are any bottles showing faded/different color, pull those bottles
and/or case.
  1.Send at most 2 “fail” bottles per batch to Modesto Winemaking – Robert
Gonzalez (address below). Please send tracking number to
Birgitta.Caspersen@ejgallo.com.
  2.Please apply a label to each bottle (hand-written) with: “Color sample”,
RDC ___ , & lot code
  3.Dispose of case(s) with missing bottles.
5.All inventory to return to FQAR (TX, AL, FL, MA) or B2-REPD (MOD). QAIC will
move to hold once complete.

ORD_LONG_TEXT:
EVENT: 101251
TYPE: P-BOX
1. Visual pallet inspection as pallets are removed form inventory and brought
to rework area for inspection
2. Inspect all four corners of each pallet.  Record location and type of damage
for each point.
3. Open and inspect each case in the pallet looking for torn, wrinkeled or
damaged outerwear cartons. - Document where in the pallet the case containing
the damaged carton comes from (bottom layer, top layer, edge, case, middle
case, etc)
4. Record lot dates of all pallets inspected.
5. Take pictures of the damage
6. Remove defective cartons (with bottle intact) and place to the side for
future rework -
7. Consolidate good cartons back into existing cases and tape closed and return
to available inventory

F2-Available.

moisture.

to rework area for inspection

future rework -

to available inventory

pulled from. Flip the case upside down and set aside for 24 hours.
•    Do not stack the cases on top of each other.
•    After 24 hours, inspect the case and bottle screw caps for any signs of
moisture.
•    Record results in the Data Collection Sheet.

reselecting each pallet. This will provide us with information on whether the
leakers Christmas-treed (leaked from the top and spread downward).
o	Either email the photos to me or upload photos here:   MOD Rework
•	Reselect for leakers (we do not need to cut open shrink wrap for all cases –
only for damaged/wet cases)
o	Good product can be released to available
•	Open each damaged/wet case and find the individual cans that are leaking –
they will likely weigh less than the others and you may be able to see the leak
 if you turn the can upside down.
•	Collect all individual leaking cans and deliver to the PSE lab c/o Marnell
Balmeo. Please note if there are multiple leaking cans from a single case.
o	For IL RDC – the cans will need to be sealed in a Ziploc bag and packed with
bubble wrap/packed nicely to prevent the cans from incurring more damage.
Please send as many of the affected cans as possible to Marnell Balmeo in Mod.
Mark the ziploc if the cans came from the same tray.

VENDORNAME: ENVASADORA DE PRODUCTOS LIDERES,
ORD_LONG_TEXT:
Reselect will include:
1.Pulling one case per pallet
2.Cutting the case half open – enough to pull out one carrier
3.Open the carrier and pull out one can to see if it is sleeved or direct
printed.
4.Return the can and carrier to the case and tape the shrink wrap.
5.Disposition the entire pallet based on the one can.
  a.Sleeved returns to Hold
  b.Direct Print goes to Available.

VENDORNAME: ENVASADORA DE PRODUCTOS LIDERES,
ORD_LONG_TEXT:
The RDC will need to pull 1 case from each of the LPNs listed in the tables
below (SKU and lot noted in the table). We’re looking to have a spread of
production times (from roughly 9am to 6pm), so what we’d like to do is pull all
 of the cases identified and then check the timestamp for each 4-pack and
select a total of 25  4-packs to provide to the lab (4 cases + one 4-pack).
Ideally, the lab would like to have at least one 4-pack per hour, but a few
4-packs from every couple of hours would be fine.
Instructions:
•	Open up cases from identified LPN and visually inspect the bottom of each
4-pk to identify the production time.
o	There is a cut-out hole in the bottom of the 4-pack that allows you to see
the timestamp
•	Select 25 out of the 36 4-packs that best represent a spread across the day
(For 10/27/22, we only need 14 out of 24 4-packs)
•	Set these 25 4-packs aside for pick up by the lab’s courier (14 4-packs for
10/27). Notify @Caspersen, Birgitta and @Hovhannisyan, Nelli once complete.
•	The remaining 11 4-packs can go to dump.
Other notes
•	They should pull out any leakers they find, report the number of affected
cases and production times, and put to damage status.
•	All unopened non-leakers should return to FQAR status.

ORD_LONG_TEXT:
1.Please remove the stretch wrap on each pallet.
2.Inspect each case on the pallet and remove any cases with Mango carriers.
a.The teams should be able to identify the different flavors without cutting
the shrink wrap open.
3.Disposition
a.Inventory with Lemon flavor on the cases can be organized onto their own
pallet and set to F2-Available status.
b.Inventory with Mango flavor indicated on the cases can be organized onto
their own pallet and set to B2-REPD status for further investigation and
sampling.
4.Please notify Herlinda.Menchaca@ejgallo.com and
Birgitta.Caspersen@ejgallo.com with the total case quantities found with mango
carriers.
